statute: Oklahoma Statutes Title 59, § 1000 et seq. (Construction Industries Board)
license_notes: Oklahoma does not require a state GC license. Electrical, mechanical, plumbing, and roofing require state licenses through Oklahoma Construction Industries Board. Local municipalities (Oklahoma City, Broken Arrow) have their own GC requirements.

mandatory_clauses.label: Contractor information
Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Electrical unlicensed = misdemeanor. Local municipality penalties vary. OK has high minimum GL insurance requirements ($500,000/$100,000).
notes: Oklahoma has no statewide GC license. Electrical, mechanical, plumbing, roofing require state licenses from CIB. Local municipalities govern GC licensing. Insurance requirements are among the highest in the nation: $500,000/$100,000 BI, $500,000/$100,000 PD. Federal 3-day notice satisfies state cancellation requirements (OK waives state notice for contracts at seller's office).
